files.00_Overview.md: Good comprehensive overview but needs restructuring: 1) Move detailed class hierarchy content to 02_Tensor_Class_Hierarchy.md to reduce redundancy, 2) Extract detailed operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, contractions) to 04_Tensor_Operations.md, 3) Extract eigen decomposition section to 05_Eigen_Decomposition.md, 4) Keep only high-level learning objectives and module summary in this file. The Overview should be a roadmap, not a deep-dive.
files.01_Introduction.md: Well-structured introduction with good 3W framework. However: 1) The Rubik's Cube analogy section (3) is over-extended and could be condensed, 2) Section 4 'Tensor Class Hierarchy' duplicates content from 02_Tensor_Class_Hierarchy.md - consider brief preview only, 3) Section 5 'Basic Tensor Operations' should move to 04_Tensor_Operations.md, 4) Keep focus on motivation, Cauchy stress concept, and principal stress analysis.
files.02_Tensor_Class_Hierarchy.md: Good technical depth but: 1) Missing Learning Objectives section at the start, 2) Missing Key Takeaways summary at the end, 3) The mermaid diagram is good but could be more comprehensive (add inheritance arrows), 4) Consider adding a 'When to use which tensor type' decision flowchart.
files.03_Storage_and_Symmetry.md: Technically sound but needs 3W enhancement: 1) Missing clear Learning Objectives, 2) Section on 'Mathematical Representation' could have better physical intuition (Why section), 3) Template Specialization section is good but needs more 'How' examples, 4) Add Key Takeaways section summarizing memory optimization strategies.
files.04_Tensor_Operations.md: Comprehensive but: 1) Missing Learning Objectives section, 2) Missing Key Takeaways, 3) The mermaid diagram showing operation flow is excellent - consider adding more visual aids for operator precedence, 4) Section 6 'Tensor Calculus' (fvc::grad, fvc::div) could be expanded with more OpenFOAM case examples.
files.05_Eigen_Decomposition.md: SEVERELY UNDERDEVELOPED - This file is a skeleton compared to others. Needs: 1) Proper Learning Objectives (What), 2) Mathematical motivation (Why eigen analysis matters in CFD), 3) Step-by-step OpenFOAM implementation (How), 4) Physical interpretation section (principal stresses, turbulence anisotropy), 5) Detailed code examples with real OpenFOAM solver references, 6) Common pitfalls section, 7) Key Takeaways, 8) Proper mermaid diagrams showing eigenvalue decomposition process.
files.06_Common_Pitfalls.md: Excellent content structure but: 1) Missing explicit Learning Objectives, 2) Could benefit from a 'Pitfall Detection Decision Tree' mermaid diagram, 3) Section 9 'Best Practices' should be expanded with code examples, 4) Consider adding a 'Real-world Case Study' section showing how a pitfall caused a simulation failure.
files.07_Summary_and_Exercises.md: SEVERELY UNDERDEVELOPED - This is supposed to be the capstone file but is minimal. Needs: 1) Comprehensive module summary connecting all concepts, 2) Progressive exercises (Basic → Intermediate → Advanced), 3) Real-world mini-project (e.g., 'Implement a custom turbulence model using tensor operations'), 4) Solutions/hints section, 5) Further reading references, 6) Self-assessment checklist, 7) Connections to next modules.

global_strategy: 1) Apply consistent 3W Framework (What/Why/How) to ALL files - currently inconsistent, 2) Eliminate redundancy: Move detailed technical content from Overview/Introduction to dedicated files, 3) Strengthen the two skeleton files (05_Eigen_Decomposition.md and 07_Summary_and_Exercises.md) to match the depth of other files, 4) Add visual learning aids (mermaid diagrams) consistently across files, 5) Ensure every file has Learning Objectives at start and Key Takeaways at end, 6) Create more cross-references between files to show learning progression, 7) Add practical 'From Math to OpenFOAM Code' translation examples throughout.
